summary: Introduces Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN), a semi-supervised node classification method using a first-order spectral approximation for efficient graph convolution.
Introduces GraphSAGE, an inductive node embedding method using neighborhood sampling and learned aggregation functions to generate representations for unseen nodes.
Proposes fast localized spectral filtering on graphs using Chebyshev polynomial approximations to enable efficient spectral graph convolutions.
Introduces spectral networks, a foundational framework for applying convolutions to graphs via Fourier analysis and spectral graph theory.
Introduces the message passing neural network (MPNN) framework, a unified abstraction for designing graph neural networks through local message passing and aggregation.
Proposes Adam, an adaptive learning rate optimization algorithm for stochastic gradient descent training of deep neural networks.
Introduces Gated Graph Sequence Neural Networks (GGNN), a recurrent architecture with gated recurrent units for learning on graphs and sequences.
Proposes convolutional networks on graphs for learning molecular fingerprints through learnable neighborhood convolutions and aggregation operations.
Introduces the Graph Neural Network model, a foundational framework for constructing neural networks on graphs using message-passing over graph structures.
Introduces PATCHY-SAN, a method that transforms graphs into grid-like structures to apply convolutional neural networks for graph classification and node ordering.
Introduces the Transformer architecture with scaled dot-product multi-head attention mechanisms for sequence-to-sequence learning.
Introduces DiffPool, a differentiable pooling method for hierarchical graph representation learning and graph classification via soft cluster assignment.
Introduces node2vec, a scalable unsupervised method for learning node embeddings using biased random walks to explore network neighborhoods.
Provides a comprehensive survey of geometric deep learning, establishing frameworks and concepts for deep learning on non-Euclidean graph-structured data.
Introduces PinSage, a GCN-based recommender system for web-scale applications combining neighborhood sampling with curriculum-based negative sampling.
Introduces LINE, a large-scale network embedding method that optimizes first-order and second-order proximities for efficient node representation learning.

Introduces Graph Isomorphism Networks (GIN), establishing connections between GNN expressiveness and the Weisfeiler-Lehman test and demonstrating that sum pooling achieves maximal discriminative power.
Introduces the skip-gram model and negative sampling method for efficient training of word embeddings.
Demonstrates breakthrough performance of deep convolutional neural networks on large-scale image classification, establishing CNNs as foundational architecture for structured data.
Proposes diffusion-convolutional neural networks as a spatial-domain approach to graph convolution using local diffusion processes.
Provides a comprehensive survey of graph neural network methods, architectures, and applications in deep learning on graphs.
Presents a unified framework for graph networks and message passing, establishing foundational principles for how neural networks generalize to relational and graph-structured data.
Introduces LeNet and foundational convolutional neural network architectures for handwritten digit recognition on grid-structured data.
Proposes DGCNN with SortPool for link prediction tasks using graph neural networks.
Introduces Neural Collaborative Filtering (NCF), a neural network approach for learning user and item embeddings from interaction data for recommendation.
Provides a comprehensive survey of graph representation learning methods and their applications.
Introduces Deep Graph Infomax (DGI), a method for learning node representations through mutual information maximization between graph views.
Presents PyTorch Geometric, a library for fast graph neural network implementation with GPU acceleration.
Identifies and addresses pitfalls in graph neural network evaluation protocols and benchmark construction.
Introduces SplineCNN, a geometric deep learning method using continuous B-spline kernels for graph and shape convolution.
Presents Relational Graph Convolutional Networks (R-GCN) for modeling multi-relational data and knowledge graphs.
Introduces Edge-Conditioned Convolution (ECC), a graph convolutional method with dynamic filters conditioned on edge attributes.
Introduces Self-Attention Graph Pooling (SAGPool), a graph pooling method that uses self-attention to select nodes for hierarchical graph coarsening.
Provides a comprehensive review of graph neural network methods, taxonomy of propagation mechanisms, and analysis of the over-smoothing phenomenon in GNNs.
Introduces Gated Recurrent Units (GRUs) as a gated recurrent variant of RNNs for sequence modeling using an encoder-decoder framework.
Proposes FastGCN, a method for efficient GCN training using layer-wise importance sampling for mini-batch gradient estimation.
Introduces Heterogeneous Graph Attention Network (HAN), an attention-based method for heterogeneous graphs using meta-paths and semantic-level attention.
Proposes Spatio-temporal Graph Convolutional Neural Network (STGCN) combining 1D convolutions and graph convolutions for traffic forecasting on spatial-temporal graphs.
Presents QM9, a large-scale database of quantum chemical properties for 134 thousand molecules with computed structural and electronic features.
Introduces Session-based Recommendation with Graph Neural Networks (SR-GNN), applying GNNs to session graphs constructed from user click sequences for recommendation.
Introduces LGCN, a large-scale learnable graph convolutional network using top-k convolution for node classification and representation learning.
Presents Cluster-GCN, an efficient training algorithm for deep graph convolutional networks that addresses memory bottlenecks through clustering-based subgraph sampling.
Introduces Dynamic Graph CNN and the EdgeCNN edge convolution operator for learning on point clouds and 3D data.
Introduces GraphRec, the first GNN-based approach to social recommendation leveraging user-item and social graphs.
Presents a node-labeling GNN framework for few-shot image classification tasks.
Contributes the Amazon Computers and Amazon Photo datasets constructed from Amazon product co-purchasing networks.
Introduces Graph U-Nets, a graph classification method combining graph pooling with convolutional operations.
Presents a knowledge graph convolutional network for recommender systems using personalized relation scoring and unified user-item knowledge graphs.
Applies graph neural networks to RGBD semantic segmentation using k-nearest neighbor graph construction for visual recognition tasks.
Proposes a graph neural network approach for session-based recommendation by converting item sequences into directed graphs and incorporating self-loop item embeddings.
Introduces gated attention networks combining gated mechanisms with attention for learning on large and spatiotemporal graphs.
Extends graph convolutional networks to handle signed graphs with positive and negative edge relationships.
Develops higher-order graph neural networks with connections to the Weisfeiler-Leman graph isomorphism test and message-passing expressiveness.
Proposes adaptive graph convolutional neural networks using kernel-based distance metrics for improved spectral graph convolution.
Presents a probabilistic generative approach to graph processing and embedding learning through contextual graph Markov models.
Proposes a spatial-temporal-preference graph attention network with distance-based edge weighting for next point-of-interest recommendation.
Introduces Global Context Enhanced Graph Neural Networks (GCE-GNN), which combines session and global graphs for session-based recommendation.
Presents a deep graph neural network method for social recommendation that leverages social relationships.
Introduces XGNN, a model-level explanation method for understanding the behavior of graph neural networks.
Proposes Attention Based Spatial-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks for capturing dynamic spatial dependencies and temporal periodicity in traffic forecasting.
Presents Nettack, a method for generating targeted adversarial attacks against graph neural networks.
Describes PyTorch, a deep learning library providing imperative-style programming and high-performance computation.
Introduces Star Graph Neural Networks with hierarchical attention for constructing enriched graph structures in session-based recommendation.
Proposes Graph Neural Networks with convolutional ARMA filters, combining autoregressive moving average techniques with graph convolution.
Introduces multi-dimensional graph convolutional networks extending GCN architectures to handle different types of graph structures.
Proposes a self-supervised hypergraph convolutional network approach for social recommendation that leverages multi-channel hypergraph structure.
Presents a multi-range attentive bicomponent graph convolutional network for traffic forecasting using GNN-based methods.
Introduces heterogeneous graph neural networks for aggregating neighbors in graph-structured data with dependency considerations.
Provides a fair comparison framework for graph neural networks on graph classification tasks, addressing experimental design concerns.
Proposes graph convolutional networks with eigendecomposition-based pooling for graph classification applications.
Presents a knowledge-enhanced hierarchical graph transformer network for multi-behavior recommendation systems.
Proposes a continuous-time sequential recommendation method using temporal graph construction and collaborative transformer.
Introduces a sequential recommendation approach using graph neural networks with attention mechanisms to weight neighbor contributions.
Proposes a hierarchical graph neural network method for social recommendation that combines non-linearity in its aggregation approach.
Presents a framework for session-based social recommendation using heterogeneous graphs and two-level attention mechanisms.
Develops instance-level explanation methods for graph neural networks through analysis of relevant walks in the graph structure.
Applies graph convolutional networks to bundle recommendation by modeling bundle-item relationships as a graph.
Introduces methods for determining adaptive propagation steps in deeper graph neural networks to improve receptive field control.
Develops temporal augmentation techniques for graph neural networks applied to session-based recommendation systems.
Proposes neighbor-interaction-aware graph convolution for recommendation that uses fixed-size neighbor sampling in its aggregation.
Provides a taxonomic survey of explainability methods in graph neural networks, organizing GNN explanation techniques and evaluation tasks.
Surveys knowledge graph-based recommender systems, examining technologies and contributions across graph-enhanced recommendation approaches.
Presents layer-wise relevance propagation-based techniques for explaining graph convolutional network predictions at the example level.
Applies self-supervised contrastive learning to graph neural networks for improved recommendation systems.
Develops a knowledge-aware graph neural network with label smoothness regularization for knowledge graph-based recommendations.
Introduces GemNet, a universal directional graph neural network architecture designed for molecular property prediction.
Proposes SIGN, a scalable graph neural network that decouples feature transformation from propagation operations.
Surveys graph neural network applications and methods within recommender systems, covering architectures and approaches.
Introduces GAG, a global attributed graph neural network for session-based recommendation that enhances current session graphs with information from related sessions.
Presents RGCN, a robust graph convolutional network designed to defend against adversarial attacks on graph-structured data.
Provides a comprehensive survey of graph neural network methods applied to recommender systems, discussing challenges and research directions.
Introduces HAGERec, a hierarchical attention graph convolutional network that incorporates knowledge graphs for explainable recommendation.
Proposes a spatial-temporal graph neural network model for traffic flow prediction.
Analyzes graph convolutional networks through the lens of signal processing, establishing that GCN aggregation functions act as low-pass filters.
Identifies and addresses the over-smoothing problem in graph neural networks through topological analysis and proposes a regularization approach.
Introduces non-local graph neural networks extending standard graph convolution methods.
Introduces GMAN, a graph multi-attention network for traffic prediction tasks.
Presents spatial-temporal graph convolutional networks for skeleton-based action recognition.
Introduces Point-GNN, a graph neural network approach for 3D object detection in point clouds.
Introduces MAGNN, a metapath aggregated graph neural network for heterogeneous graph embedding.
Proposes E(3)-equivariant graph neural networks for data-efficient prediction of interatomic potentials.
Presents methods for learning robust graph structures within graph neural networks to improve robustness.
Develops graph neural network methods for anomaly detection in multivariate time series.
Proposes graph neural networks for multivariate time series forecasting by connecting temporal variables.
Introduces methods for learning visual commonsense to improve robustness in scene graph generation.
Proposes spatial-temporal fusion in graph neural networks for traffic flow forecasting.
Introduces an edge-labeling graph neural network approach for few-shot learning.
Presents SpookyNet, a method for learning force fields with electronic degrees of freedom and nonlocal effects.
Develops regularized graph neural networks for emotion recognition from EEG signals.
Proposes a dual graph enhanced embedding neural network for click-through rate prediction.
Introduces an atomistic line graph neural network for improved materials property prediction.
